name: Manga Dialogues: Travel & Survival
whatsNew: Five animated travel scenes to get you through a trip to China — the airport, hotel check-in, buying a train ticket, bargaining at a market and asking for help. Watch, listen, tap any word, then read it as a comic.

dialogue.chinese: 您好，请出示护照和机票。
dialogue.pinyin: Nín hǎo, qǐng chūshì hùzhào hé jīpiào.
dialogue.english: Hello, please show your passport and ticket.

dialogue.chinese: 给您。我想要靠窗的座位。
dialogue.pinyin: Gěi nín. Wǒ xiǎng yào kào chuāng de zuòwèi.
dialogue.english: Here you are. I'd like a window seat.

dialogue.chinese: 好的。您有几件行李要托运？
dialogue.pinyin: Hǎo de. Nín yǒu jǐ jiàn xíngli yào tuōyùn?
dialogue.english: Sure. How many bags are you checking in?

dialogue.pinyin: Yí jiàn. Dēngjīkǒu zài nǎlǐ?
dialogue.english: One. Where is the boarding gate?

dialogue.pinyin: Zài sānshíbā hào, qǐng zǒu zhèbiān.
dialogue.english: Gate 38 — please go this way.

dialogue.pinyin: Xièxie! Zhù wǒ yílù shùnfēng.
dialogue.english: Thanks! Wish me a smooth journey.

questions.question: What seat does the traveller want?

dialogue.pinyin: Nǐ hǎo, wǒ dìng le yí ge fángjiān.
dialogue.english: Hi, I've booked a room.

dialogue.pinyin: Qǐngwèn nín guìxìng?
dialogue.english: May I ask your surname?

dialogue.chinese: 我姓王。这是我的身份证。
dialogue.pinyin: Wǒ xìng Wáng. Zhè shì wǒ de shēnfènzhèng.
dialogue.english: My surname is Wang. Here's my ID.

dialogue.chinese: 好的，您住两晚，房间在五楼。
dialogue.pinyin: Hǎo de, nín zhù liǎng wǎn, fángjiān zài wǔ lóu.
dialogue.english: OK, two nights, your room is on the fifth floor.

dialogue.pinyin: Zǎocān jǐ diǎn kāishǐ?
dialogue.english: What time does breakfast start?

dialogue.chinese: 早上七点。这是您的房卡。
dialogue.pinyin: Zǎoshang qī diǎn. Zhè shì nín de fángkǎ.
dialogue.english: Seven a.m. Here's your key card.

questions.question: How many nights is the guest staying?

dialogue.chinese: 我要一张去上海的高铁票。
dialogue.pinyin: Wǒ yào yì zhāng qù Shànghǎi de gāotiě piào.
dialogue.english: I'd like one high-speed rail ticket to Shanghai.

dialogue.pinyin: Nín yào jǐ diǎn de?
dialogue.english: What time would you like?

dialogue.pinyin: Xiàwǔ sān diǎn zuǒyòu de.
dialogue.english: Around three in the afternoon.

dialogue.chinese: 有一趟三点十分的，二等座。
dialogue.pinyin: Yǒu yí tàng sān diǎn shí fēn de, èr děng zuò.
dialogue.english: There's one at 3:10, second class.

dialogue.pinyin: Hǎo, duōshao qián?
dialogue.english: Great, how much is it?

dialogue.pinyin: Wǔbǎi wǔshísān kuài.
dialogue.english: 553 yuan.

dialogue.pinyin: Lǎobǎn, zhège duōshao qián?
dialogue.english: Boss, how much is this?

dialogue.pinyin: Liǎngbǎi kuài, hěn piányi de.
dialogue.english: Two hundred yuan — very cheap.

dialogue.chinese: 太贵了！能不能便宜一点？
dialogue.pinyin: Tài guì le! Néng bu néng piányi yìdiǎn?
dialogue.english: Too expensive! Can you make it a bit cheaper?

dialogue.chinese: 那给你便宜一点，一百八。
dialogue.pinyin: Nà gěi nǐ piányi yìdiǎn, yìbǎi bā.
dialogue.english: OK, a bit cheaper for you — 180.

dialogue.pinyin: Yìbǎi kuài, xíng bu xíng?
dialogue.english: One hundred — is that OK?

dialogue.pinyin: Yìbǎi èr, bù néng zài shǎo le.
dialogue.english: 120 — I can't go any lower.

questions.question: What was the first price the vendor gave?
What is the vendor's final price?

dialogue.chinese: 不好意思，能帮我一下吗？
dialogue.pinyin: Bù hǎoyìsi, néng bāng wǒ yíxià ma?
dialogue.english: Excuse me, could you help me?

dialogue.pinyin: Dāngrán kěyǐ, zěnme le?
dialogue.english: Of course. What's wrong?

dialogue.chinese: 我的手机丢了，我迷路了。
dialogue.pinyin: Wǒ de shǒujī diū le, wǒ mílù le.
dialogue.english: I've lost my phone and I'm lost.

dialogue.pinyin: Bié zháojí. Nǐ yào qù nǎlǐ?
dialogue.english: Don't worry. Where do you need to go?

dialogue.pinyin: Wǒ yào huí Hépíng Fàndiàn.
dialogue.english: I need to get back to the Peace Hotel.

dialogue.pinyin: Wǒ dài nǐ qù dìtiě zhàn, hěn jìn.
dialogue.english: I'll take you to the subway station — it's close.

questions.question: What problem does the tourist have?
options: Lost phone and lost the way

questions.question: How will the passer-by help?
options: Take them to the subway station
